title: File Servers, Object Storage, CDNs, and Architecture Selection · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for file servers, object storage, cdns, and architecture selection and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for architecture and responsibility decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.
Separate control-plane metadata and authorization from high-volume data transfer while preserving one auditable decision.
Choose server upload, direct upload, redirect, proxy, signed edge delivery, or public delivery from threat, transformation, audit, and performance evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Filesystem Object Cdn · changed case · Quiz Filesystem Object Cdn
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 9564 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.

Which action demonstrates Filesystem Object Cdn without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Filesystem Object Cdn · changed case · Quiz Filesystem Object Cdn.

AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Cost Failure Model.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Cost Failure Model, keep Cost Failure Model connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Cost Failure Model, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Cost Failure Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.
